skill.name: x-article-publisher
skill.description: Publish Markdown articles to X (Twitter) Articles editor with proper formatting. Use when user wants to publish a Markdown file/URL to X Articles, or mentions "publish to X", "post article to Twitter", "X article", or wants help with X Premium article publishing. Handles cover image upload and converts Markdown to rich text automatically.
skill.summary: Publish Markdown articles to X (Twitter) Articles editor with rich text formatting
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 97 potential issues, but all are false positives. The skill uses standard Playwright browser automation and Python helper scripts for markdown parsing and clipboard operations. All external command references are documentation examples in markdown code blocks, not actual shell execution. URLs are legitimate X/Twitter endpoints. No confirmed security risks identified.

medium_findings.title: External Command Documentation (False Positive)
medium_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 67 instances of shell commands in SKILL.md. These are all documentation examples in markdown code blocks, not actual code execution. Example: `python parse_markdown.py article.md` is a documented usage example.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: All 67 occurrences are markdown code blocks containing usage examples. The skill uses Playwright MCP for browser automation, not shell execution.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs (Expected Behavior)
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged hardcoded URLs like https://x.com/compose/articles. These are legitimate X/Twitter URLs for the article editor, which is the skill's intended target.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: These URLs are expected for a skill that publishes to X Articles - they point to the official X article editor.

low_findings.title: Argparse Misidentified as Cryptography (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged argparse.ArgumentParser as 'weak cryptographic algorithm'. This is incorrect - argparse is Python's standard CLI argument parsing library, not cryptography.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: argparse.ArgumentParser is a standard CLI argument parser. No cryptographic functions are used in this skill.

low_findings.title: Standard File Paths Flagged as Hidden/Temp Access (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged ~/.claude/skills/ and /tmp/ as hidden files and temp directory access. These are standard Claude Code skill paths and normal temp file locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: ~/.claude/skills/ is the standard Claude Code skills directory. /tmp/ is a standard temp directory for intermediate file storage during markdown processing.

content.user_title: Publish to X Articles from Markdown
content.value_statement: Automate publishing Markdown articles to X (Twitter) Articles with proper rich text formatting. Converts Markdown to HTML and handles image placement automatically using block index positioning.
actual_capabilities: Parse Markdown files to extract title, cover image, and content images with block indices
Convert Markdown to HTML with preserved formatting (headers, bold, italic, links, lists, blockquotes)
Copy HTML to system clipboard for rich text paste into X editor
Copy images to clipboard with optional compression for upload
Automate X Articles editor using Playwright MCP for seamless publishing workflow
limitations: Requires X Premium Plus subscription for Articles feature
Only works with Playwright MCP for browser automation
Requires macOS for clipboard functionality (pyobjc dependency)
Cannot auto-publish - always saves as draft for user review

use_cases.title: Bloggers and Content Creators
use_cases.description: Writers who maintain blogs in Markdown can directly publish to X Articles without manual copying and formatting.

use_cases.title: News and Update Publishers
use_cases.description: Publishers who need to quickly share formatted articles on X without the X web editor's limitations.

use_cases.title: Automation Enthusiasts
use_cases.description: Users who want to integrate X Article publishing into their automated content pipelines.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Article Publishing
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the x-article-publisher skill to publish my Markdown file at /path/to/article.md to X Articles.
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple request to publish a Markdown file

prompt_templates.title: Article with Images
prompt_templates.prompt: Publish the article in /path/to/blog-post.md to X Articles. Make sure to include all images with proper positioning.
prompt_templates.scenario: Publishing an article with multiple images

prompt_templates.title: Draft for Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Parse my article at /path/to/draft.md and prepare it for X Articles. Save as draft, do not publish.
prompt_templates.scenario: Preparing a draft for manual review before publishing

prompt_templates.title: Batch Publishing
prompt_templates.prompt: I have multiple Markdown articles in /articles/ folder. Use x-article-publisher to prepare them for X Articles publishing.

output_examples.input: Publish my article at ~/blog/my-post.md to X Articles
output_examples.output: Successfully parsed Markdown file. Extracted title 'My Blog Post', cover image '~/blog/images/cover.jpg', and 3 content images. HTML copied to clipboard. Navigate to X Articles editor to paste content.

HTML output:

Section Title

Paragraph with bold and italic text.

  • List item 1
  • List item 2

best_practices: Always save as draft first - never auto-publish without user confirmation
Use block_index for image positioning instead of text matching for reliability
Prepare all data (parse markdown, generate HTML) before starting browser automation to minimize wait times
Test with a sample article first to verify image placement before processing important content
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to auto-publish - this violates X terms and user trust
Avoid using text matching for image positioning - block_index is more reliable
Do not skip the draft review step - always let user verify before publishing
Avoid excessive browser_wait_for calls - most elements are immediately available after page load

faq.question: Does this skill require X Premium?
faq.answer: Yes, X Articles feature requires X Premium Plus subscription. The skill will prompt you if login is needed.

faq.question: Can I auto-publish articles?
faq.answer: No, the skill is designed to always save as draft for user review. Auto-publishing is disabled for safety.

faq.question: What Markdown features are supported?
faq.answer: Headers (H2, H3), bold, italic, links, ordered/unordered lists, blockquotes, and images are supported. Code blocks are converted to blockquotes.

faq.question: How does image positioning work?
faq.answer: The skill uses block_index - each image has an index indicating which block element it should be inserted after. This is more reliable than text matching.

faq.question: What platforms are supported?
faq.answer: The skill works on macOS due to clipboard dependencies (pyobjc). The Python scripts can run on any platform but clipboard copying requires macOS.

faq.question: Can I use this with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill is designed for Claude Code and uses Playwright MCP for browser automation. It works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
